channel.title: IPUMS DHS Spatial Analysis and Health Research Hub
channel.description: Understand population health in its environmental context

item.title: Access to Healthcare Part 3
item.dc:creator: Grace Cooper
item.description: Spatial accessibility to healthcare, a known barrier to healthcare utilization1 which leads to poor health2, is understudied in the regions that need it most, such as rural sub-Saharan Africa, due to lack of adequate road network and population data. Spatial accessibility models that account for more complex influences on spatial access, such as distance decay and supply and demand of services (i.e. gravity models such as the Two-Step Floating Catchment Area (2SFCA) model), typically depend on vector-based data that is less available in areas with the most vulnerable populations. Vector-based data include census population counts aggregated to administrative units and road network data. Global gridded population datasets (publicly available, high resolution population estimates), are becoming more widely used in social and population health studies3, however they continue to be underutilized in spatial accessibility to health models4–6. This post describes an innovative method researchers can use to incorporate gridded population, land cover, and elevation data into a 2SFCA model for use in any region in the world, regardless of administrative data availability.

item.title: Access to Healthcare Part 2: The 2SFCA Model
item.dc:creator: Grace Cooper
item.description: Health outcomes and healthcare utilization rates are influenced by spatial accessibility to health, especially in rural areas of developing countries. This second post in a series of three describes one of the most effective ways to measure spatial accessibility: the Two-Step Floating Catchment Area (2SFCA) Model.

item.title: Access to Healthcare Part 1: How it is Measured
item.dc:creator: Grace Cooper
item.description: Many people in developing countries experience poor health as a result of poor spatial access to clinics and hospitals. Our understanding of how to improve global health is limited by our ability to measure healthcare accessibility.

item.title: Vegetation and Land Cover Part 3: Land Cover/Land Use
item.dc:creator: Devon Kristiansen
item.description: An almost overwhelming number of vegetative and land cover data types exist. This post describes and compares sources of land cover data, as the final installment of a series of three.

item.title: Vegetation and Land Cover Part 2: Environmental Moisture
item.dc:creator: Devon Kristiansen
item.description: An almost overwhelming number of vegetative and land cover data types exist. This second post of three describes a few common indices representing vegetative or soil moisture, with an emphasis on agricultural productivity.

item.title: Vegetation and Land Cover Part 1: Vegetative Indices
item.dc:creator: Devon Kristiansen
item.description: An almost overwhelming number of vegetative and land cover data types exist. As the first post in a series of three, this blog gives a brief primer into vegetative indices and which ones might be most appropriate for different research goals.

item.title: Multilevel Dietary Diversity using DHS data
item.dc:creator: Devon Kristiansen
item.description: A follow-up post on using a comparable measure of dietary diversity in a multilevel model including NDVI contextual information in the model.

item.title: Using Historical and Projected Climate Data to Forecast Child Malnutrition
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Luttinen
Jessie Pinchoff
item.description: In the final post in our series on future estimation we create a model to predict child malnutrition and plug in projected climate scenario data to predict a future level of child malnutrition.

item.title: Harnessing CHC-CMIP6 Climate Scenario Data to Explore the Future
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Luttinen
Jessie Pinchoff
item.description: Returning to our series on future estimation, we provide an overview of a data source for future climate scenarios.

item.title: Measuring Dietary Diversity using DHS data
item.dc:creator: Devon Kristiansen
Rebecca Luttinen
item.description: An introduction to constructing comparable dietary diversity indicators. This post breaks away from the series on future conditions, with the first post of two on dietary diversity.

item.title: Estimating the now and predicting the future: Fertility rate estimation and population projection
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Luttinen
Elizabeth Heger Boyle
item.description: Use the DHS to estimate fertility levels and project future population levels

item.title: Anticipating the Future: Important terminology
item.dc:creator: Molly Brown
Rebecca Luttinen
item.description: Part 1 in our series exploring climate and population projections in health research

item.title: From MODIS to VIIRS: The Latest Source for NDVI Data
item.dc:creator: Finn Roberts
Rebecca Luttinen
item.description: Update your NDVI workflows with NASA’s newest instruments

item.title: Incorporating Qualitative Methods into Spatial Health Research
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Grace
item.description: Spatiotemporal research is enhanced when guided by local knowledge, perspective, and expertise.

item.title: Using QGIS for Spatial Data Analysis
item.dc:creator: Ankit Sikarwar
item.description: Standalone GIS software can provide an interactive alternative to code-based analysis workflows

item.title: Using Leaflet for Exploratory Data Analysis: Part 2
item.dc:creator: Finn Roberts
item.description: Exploring customization options for leaflet maps

item.title: Using Leaflet for Exploratory Data Analysis: Part 1
item.dc:creator: Finn Roberts
item.description: An introduction to interactive mapping using the leaflet package

item.title: Measuring Population Density
item.dc:creator: Grace Cooper
item.description: An introduction to gridded data sources used to measure human presence across the globe

item.title: Aggregation Methods for NDVI Data
item.dc:creator: Finn Roberts
item.description: We use data from two regions to illustrate approaches to processing NDVI data

item.title: An Iterative Workflow for Loading NDVI Data in R
item.dc:creator: Finn Roberts
item.description: Use purrr and terra to efficiently integrate NDVI data across multiple spatial extents
